workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30–45° and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your upper back and head are supported (supine on the incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Bring the dumbbells up to shoulder level with palms facing forward and elbows bent (upper arms roughly 45° from the torso) while retracting the shoulder blades slightly.
Unrack the dumbbells by pressing them up and then lowering them to start position so the handles are beside your upper chest/shoulder area.
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly straight (avoid locking or hyperextending the elbows); exhale during the press.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then slowly lower the dumbbells under control back to the upper chest/shoulder level while inhaling.
Keep a stable core and avoid excessive arching of the lower back; maintain scapular control and smooth, controlled reps for the desired number of repetitions.
If using heavy weights, have a spotter or perform a controlled seated dumbbell rack/unrack to avoid strain when starting or finishing the set.
exercises.description: A pressing movement performed lying supine on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest. Dumbbells allow a slightly greater range of motion and independent arm movement compared with a barbell.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Back Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Back Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Low Seated Row
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the bench or seat with your feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; reach forward and grasp the low cable handle with a neutral or overhand grip.
instructions: Sit upright on the bench or seat with your feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; reach forward and grasp the low cable handle with a neutral or overhand grip.
Start with your torso leaning slightly forward at the hips, chest up, shoulders relaxed and spine neutral — this is the starting position.
Exhale and pull the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving your elbows straight back, leading the movement with your elbows rather than your hands.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the pull and hold the peak contraction for a brief count, keeping your torso stable (avoid excessive leaning or momentum).
Inhale and slowly extend your arms to return the handle to the starting position with control, allowing the lats and upper back to lengthen fully.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine, steady tempo, and controlled range of motion throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable low row performed from a bench with feet braced; you pull the handle toward your torso to drive scapular retraction and elbow extension, focusing on the muscles of the back. Controlled return lengthens the lats and upper back while the arms and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Cable Low Seated Row with V bar
exercises.notes: Sit tall with a slight forward lean from the hips, chest lifted, core engaged and shoulders relaxed; allow the arms to fully extend so the weight is supported by the arms and scapulae are slightly protracted.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the low cable pulley with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the V‑bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Sit tall with a slight forward lean from the hips, chest lifted, core engaged and shoulders relaxed; allow the arms to fully extend so the weight is supported by the arms and scapulae are slightly protracted.
Initiate the movement by pulling the elbows straight back toward your torso (not flaring wide), driving the elbows past your sides and pulling the V‑bar to your lower chest/upper abdomen.
At the end range, squeeze your shoulder blades together and hold the contraction for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable and upright.
Slowly extend the arms forward in a controlled manner, allowing the scapulae to re‑protract and return to the start position without rounding the lower back.
Breathe out while pulling, inhale while returning; avoid using momentum or leaning excessively backward—keep the motion controlled and driven by the back and arms.
exercises.description: Seated low cable row using a V‑bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the middle/upper back by driving the elbows back and squeezing the shoulder blades, while also engaging the lats and arm muscles. The neutral V‑grip shortens the lever and increases biceps involvement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Shoulder Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Shoulder Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward (or slightly rotated) and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees, wrists stacked over elbows.
Inhale, then exhale as you press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly extended overhead; avoid fully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulders down and chest lifted (do not shrug excessively).
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Keep the back pressed against the bench and avoid excessive lower-back arching or leaning; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a back-supported bench to drive the weights overhead, primarily working the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with triceps and upper traps assisting. The movement emphasizes vertical pressing strength and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Cable Upright Row
exercises.notes: Set a low pulley with a straight bar or short double-handle attachment and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a low pulley with a straight bar or short double-handle attachment and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees; brace your core and keep the chest up.
Grasp the bar with an overhand (pronated) grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower, arms extended down toward the pulley.
Start the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the bar close to your body and leading with the elbows.
Continue pulling until your elbows reach about shoulder height or the bar comes to the upper chest/clavicle area, keeping wrists relatively neutral.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but avoiding excessive shrugging or neck tension.
Lower the bar slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms while maintaining core tension.
Breathe out as you pull up and inhale as you lower; maintain steady tempo and avoid using torso momentum or excessive back lean.
exercises.description: A standing cable exercise that raises a close-grip bar vertically toward the upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (delts) while also engaging the traps and arm muscles. Performed from a low or mid pulley, it provides constant tension through the range of motion.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with feet flat about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor (or a 30–45° decline), keep a neutral spine and neck, and brace your abs.
Allow the dumbbells to hang beneath your shoulders with a slight bend in the elbows; maintain that elbow angle throughout the set.

Exhale and lift the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc by externally rotating the shoulders and squeezing the shoulder blades together; raise until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the top focusing on contracting the rear delts and upper back, then inhale and lower the weights slowly back to the starting position with control.
Avoid swinging the torso or using momentum, do not shrug the shoulders up toward the ears, and keep the movement driven by the shoulders/scapulae rather than the lower back.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell rear fly is a bent-over horizontal shoulder abduction movement that targets the rear delts and upper back by pulling the arms out to the sides while keeping the torso stable. It strengthens posterior shoulder muscles and scapular retractors with a controlled, rowing-like motion.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Arm Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Arm Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (with rope attachment)
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a high pulley with a rope attachment and select an appropriate weight.
Stand upright facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip so palms face each other and thumbs point inward; elbows should be tucked at your sides and forearms pointing forward with the upper arms nearly vertical.
Inhale, then exhale as you extend your elbows to push the rope downward until your arms are fully straight; at the bottom, separate the rope ends slightly outward to maximize triceps contraction.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping the elbows stationary and wrists neutral.
Slowly control the return (eccentric) phase by allowing the forearms to rise back to the start position until forearms are approximately parallel to the floor or at about 90° elbow flexion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while avoiding torso sway, shoulder elevation, or elbow travel forward/backward.
If you feel shoulder or lower-back compensation, reduce weight, shorten range slightly, or keep a more upright torso and tighter core brace.
exercises.description: Standing cable rope pushdowns isolate the triceps by extending the elbow against a high-pulley resistance; the rope attachment allows a neutral grip and slight outward flare at full extension to emphasize all triceps heads. Keep the elbows fixed at the sides and move only at the elbow joint for controlled concentric and eccentric phases.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward (supinated grip) and arms fully extended.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and fixed in position throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until the weights reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining upright posture and avoid leaning back or using momentum.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with full elbow extension under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on strict form and minimizing shoulder or torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell biceps curl isolates the biceps by flexing the elbow against resistance while standing upright; perform controlled reps without swinging to maximize tension on the biceps.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
instructions: Sit upright near the edge of a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet flat on the floor (or on a small block for increased range of motion).
Place a barbell across your upper thighs just above the knees; use a pad if needed for comfort and grip the bar with both hands to steady it (seated position).
Position the balls of your feet on the floor (or a small raised platform) while keeping heels hanging or supported so your ankles can move through full range.
Keeping your torso upright and core engaged, exhale and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.

Pause briefly at the top with the calves fully contracted, then inhale and slowly lower the heels back down until you feel a stretch in the calves but maintain control (avoid letting the weight slam down).
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; maintain a steady tempo, avoid bouncing, and keep knees stationary throughout the set.
To stop, carefully roll the barbell forward off your thighs or have a spotter help lift it off before standing.
exercises.description: Seated on a bench with a loaded barbell resting across the thighs, raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles to target the calf muscles. The movement is performed with knees bent, emphasizing the soleus portion of the calves through controlled heel raises and lowers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight.
instructions: Set up a barbell in a squat rack at shoulder height and load desired weight. Step under the bar and place it across your upper back/traps, grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Unrack the bar and step back from the rack, then position an elevated platform or weight plate under the balls of your feet so your heels can hang off the edge; stand with feet hip-width apart.
Brace your core (abs) and maintain an upright torso; keep knees slightly soft (not fully locked) throughout the movement.
Exhale and press through the balls of both feet to raise your heels as high as possible, coming up onto your toes and fully contracting the calves.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the calves, keeping the barbell stable on your traps and upper back (traps engaged to support the load).
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the level of the platform until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then carefully step forward and rack the barbell with control or use a spotter/safety pins if needed.
exercises.description: Standing calf raise performed with a barbell across the upper back; raise heels by plantarflexing at the ankle to work the calf muscles, then lower heels below the platform for a stretch. The barbell and core help stabilize the body during each repetition.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

workout.title: Upper Body - STRENGTH
workout.description: Upper Body workout focusing on strength training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell High Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about upper-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about upper-chest height. Step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar position), not on the neck.
Grip the bar just outside shoulder width and retract your shoulder blades to create a stable shelf; lift the bar off the rack and take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack.
Stand with feet approximately shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward. Brace your abs and keep a tall, upright torso.
Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, keeping weight distributed through the midfoot to heels.
Descend under control until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows), ensuring knees track over the toes and do not collapse inward.
Maintain chest up and a neutral spine throughout the descent; keep the upper back tight to support the bar.
Drive through the heels and midfoot, extend the knees and hips simultaneously to return to a standing position, exhaling as you rise.
Reset your brace at the top, then repeat for the desired number of repetitions. When finished, step forward into the rack and safely re-rack the bar.
exercises.description: A barbell high bar squat places the bar on the upper traps and emphasizes knee flexion to target the quads while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings. The lifter descends to at least parallel with an upright torso, then drives back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Sumo Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a squat-like setup (upright torso, hips lower than a conventional deadlift) and grip the bar with hands inside your knees using a double overhand or mixed grip.
instructions: Stand with feet well outside shoulder width (sumo stance), toes pointed slightly outward; position the bar over the mid-foot while standing upright.

Hinge at the hips and bend the knees to lower into a squat-like setup (upright torso, hips lower than a conventional deadlift) and grip the bar with hands inside your knees using a double overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core and keep a neutral spine (no rounded back); pull your shoulder blades down and back so the lats are engaged and chest is up.
Drive your knees out so they track over your toes (toes and knees aligned) to engage the adductors and maintain the wide stance.
Initiate the lift by pushing through the heels and mid-foot, driving the hips forward and straightening the legs simultaneously—keep the bar close to the shins.
Stand tall at lockout by fully extending the hips and knees, squeezing the glutes and keeping the shoulders back (avoid hyperextending the lower back).
Lower the bar under control by hinging at the hips first, then bending the knees to return the bar to the floor while maintaining a neutral spine and tight core.
Reset your position between reps as needed (re-brace core, realign knees over toes, and ensure bar remains over mid-foot) before initiating the next repetition.
exercises.description: The barbell sumo deadlift is a wide-stance deadlift variation that emphasizes hip extension and inner-thigh drive to lift the bar from the floor. It uses a more upright torso and wider foot placement to shift load onto the glutes and adductors while still recruiting the posterior chain and legs.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Press Squat
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
instructions: Start standing with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward and a dumbbell held vertically at chest in a goblet grip (hands around the head/handle), elbows close to your body.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders down and back, keep chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
Inhale and hinge at the hips while bending the knees to lower into a squat — push hips back and let knees track over toes until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you rise and maintaining a strong upright torso.
Once standing, press the dumbbell overhead (or slightly forward depending on comfort), extending the elbows and engaging delts/pectorals; exhale during the press.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the goblet position at your chest with control, resetting your brace before the next descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movements steady, knees tracking toes and avoiding rounded lower back or knees collapsing inward.
exercises.description: A standing compound movement that combines a goblet-style squat with a dumbbell press; it primarily works the quads while also engaging the chest, shoulders and arms. Perform by squatting from a goblet hold and then pressing the weight as you stand.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.
Prioritize form over weight - quality reps build strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell resting on each thigh.
Lie back so you are supine on the bench, feet flat on the floor, knees bent, and maintain a neutral spine with a slight natural arch; retract and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Use your legs and core to help bring the dumbbells up to chest height so your upper arms are about 45 degrees from your torso and palms are facing forward (or slightly inward if preferred).

Inhale, then slowly lower the dumbbells in a controlled manner until your elbows are roughly level with the bench or just below chest level, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking slightly tucked.

Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulder blade position, then exhale and press the dumbbells upward and slightly together until your arms are almost fully extended — avoid locking the elbows.
Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
To finish the set, bring the dumbbells safely to your knees, sit up, and set them down or have a spotter assist if using heavy weight.
exercises.description: The dumbbell bench press is a supine pressing movement performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and delts for elbow and shoulder extension. Dumbbells allow a greater range of motion and require more stabilization from the forearms and serratus anterior.

exercises.name: Cable Neutral Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat and your thighs are secured under the pads while seated upright. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and thigh pads so your feet are flat and your thighs are secured under the pads while seated upright.
Attach a neutral (parallel) handle and grasp it with both hands so your palms face each other; sit tall with a slight lean back (~10–20°), chest up, and shoulders down and back.
Start with your arms fully extended overhead and shoulder blades slightly protracted; brace your core to keep the torso stable.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows down and back, pulling the handle toward your upper chest / clavicle region while keeping your wrists neutral.
Squeeze your lats and pinch your shoulder blades together at the bottom of the movement for a brief 1‑2 second hold.
Return the handle slowly and under control to the start position, allowing your arms to fully extend and your shoulder blades to re‑protract.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding excessive torso lean, momentum, or shrugging the shoulders; keep the movement smooth and elbow-driven.
If you feel the biceps doing all the work, reduce the weight and focus on leading with the elbows and actively contracting the lats.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a neutral (parallel) grip that emphasizes the latissimus dorsi while reducing stress on the shoulders; the bar is pulled down toward the upper chest with elbow-driven movement. Performed seated with thighs braced under pads, focusing on a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height so your chest rests firmly against the vertical pad and your feet are flat on the floor. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Adjust the seat height so your chest rests firmly against the vertical pad and your feet are flat on the floor.
Select an appropriate weight on the lever machine and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, arms extended in front at about shoulder height.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows, maintain a neutral spine and neck, and brace your core throughout the movement.
Initiate the rep by retracting the shoulder blades and driving your upper arms out to the sides in a controlled arc until your elbows (or upper arms) are roughly parallel with the floor.
Pause and squeeze the rear delts and upper back at the end range for 1–2 seconds while keeping the torso stable against the pad.
Slowly return the handles to the start position under control, resisting the weight; inhale on the return, exhale as you pull.
Avoid shrugging the shoulders or using momentum; keep movement smooth and focused on the delts and upper back.
exercises.description: Seated reverse fly on a lever machine that isolates the rear deltoids by moving the upper arms out and back in a horizontal plane while the chest is braced against the pad. The movement emphasizes scapular retraction and posterior shoulder activation.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down from your shoulders with your arms fully extended; keep your head aligned with your spine.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows back and up, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs or hip area while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, maintaining tightness through the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled motion until your arms are extended.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping the torso fixed (avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation) and continue to breathe: exhale while pulling, inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with dumbbells where you hinge at the hips and pull the weights toward your torso, emphasizing the lats and upper back. The movement also engages the arms and posterior chain to stabilize the hinged position.

exercises.name: Barbell Full squat (with rack)
exercises.notes: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set the safety pins and rack J-hooks so the bar sits at about mid-chest height when standing; load the bar and secure collars.
Stand under the bar with it resting across your upper back/traps (not on the neck). Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs to lift it off the hooks, then take 1–2 small steps back so the rack is clear; stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core (tighten abs), keep chest up and eyes forward. Inhale and begin the descent by pushing hips back and bending knees, keeping knees tracking over toes.
Lower to full depth (hips at or below parallel to the knees) while maintaining a neutral spine and weight distributed through the mid-foot and heels.
Drive through the heels and mid-foot, exhale, and extend hips and knees to return to a fully upright standing position while maintaining a tight core and upright torso.
Step forward and carefully re-rack the bar on the hooks. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat performed from a rack where the lifter unracks the bar, steps back and squats to full depth (hips at or below parallel) before returning to standing. This compound lower-body movement primarily loads the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip). Stop 1 rep short of failure (RPE 9). Spinal safety takes priority.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward or slightly out, barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
Set a neutral spine (no rounding), pull shoulder blades down and back, lift the chest and brace the abs tightly.
Take a deep breath, drive through the heels and mid-foot, extend the hips and knees together to lift the bar, keeping the bar close to the shins and thighs.
Continue driving the hips forward until standing tall with hips fully extended and shoulders stacked over hips (lockout); do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with glutes engaged and core braced.
To lower, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back while maintaining a neutral spine, let the bar pass the knees, then bend the knees and place the bar back on the floor under control.
Reset breathing and core tension before each repetition and keep consistent form; avoid rounding the spine and jerking the bar.
exercises.description: A heavy compound hip-hinge lift that develops the glutes and the posterior chain by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing position. The movement also requires strong spinal bracing and upper-back stability while challenging grip strength.

exercises.name: Lever Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Stand upright on the machine platform with the balls of both feet on the edge and heels hanging off; position your shoulders under the padded lever and grasp the handles.
Unlatch or release the safety so the weight rests on the shoulders and stand tall with a neutral spine and knees slightly bent (not locked).
Inhale, then exhale as you press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, squeezing the calves at the top.
Hold the top position briefly (1 second) while maintaining an upright torso and stable hips.
Inhale as you slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves.
Control the descent—avoid bouncing—and repeat for desired reps, then carefully re-rack or secure the lever when finished.
exercises.description: A bilateral standing calf raise performed on a lever/shoulder machine that isolates the calf muscles through plantarflexion. The exercise raises the heels off a platform, then lowers them below platform level for a full stretch.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Allow full recovery between exercises - this is not cardio.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs.

Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back. Retract your shoulder blades and keep them contacting the bench.
Start with the dumbbells at mid-chest, elbows bent about 90 degrees and wrists neutral (dumbbell handles aligned with forearms).
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended; keep wrists stacked over elbows.

Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position, maintaining control and stopping when the dumbbells reach chest level or your elbows form ~90 degrees. Keep elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from your torso to protect the shoulders.
Keep your core braced and feet firmly planted for stability throughout each rep; avoid excessive flaring of the ribs or letting the shoulders lift off the bench.
Use a spotter for heavy loads and choose a weight that allows controlled full-range reps with good form.
exercises.description: Flat dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a bench, pressing two dumbbells upward from chest level to develop the pectorals; the movement also recruits the anterior delts and triceps for stability and lockout.

exercises.name: Pull-up
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
instructions: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
Stand facing the bar and grasp it with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Mount the machine and kneel on the pad with knees supported and feet either crossed or tucked behind; allow the arms to fully extend so your body hangs straight.
Brace your core, keep your head neutral, and pull your shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement.
Exhale and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down and back, bringing your chin or upper chest toward the bar while squeezing the lats and upper back.
Pause briefly at the top with the scapulae retracted and avoid excessive shrugging of the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower yourself under control until your arms are fully extended and shoulders are not shrugged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; reduce assistance (use a lighter counterweight) as you get stronger to increase difficulty.
exercises.description: Assisted pull-up on a counterweight machine where the user kneels on the support pad and pulls their body up to the bar; this exercise primarily targets the lats while also working the pulling muscles of the arms and upper back.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell lateral raise works the lateral delts by lifting the arms out to the sides to about shoulder height, with a slight elbow bend and controlled motion. It improves shoulder width and isolates the middle shoulder fibers while the traps and forearms assist.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip.
Hinge at the hips, push your butt back and keep a slight bend in the knees until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor; maintain a neutral spine and neck (bent-over position).
Let the arms hang straight down from the shoulders with a small bend in the elbows; keep the shoulder blades relaxed but ready to retract.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc (horizontal abduction) leading with the elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and at shoulder height.
Squeeze the rear delts and pinch the shoulder blades together at the top for a brief pause.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, avoiding momentum or torso swing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement controlled and the torso stable throughout.
exercises.description: A bent-over dumbbell fly that isolates the posterior (rear) deltoids by horizontally abducting the arms from a hinged position. Performed with a hip hinge and slight elbow bend, the movement emphasizes the rear delts while engaging the upper back and traps to stabilize and retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides (neutral grip, palms facing the body).
Keep a neutral spine and neck, chest up, and arms straight but not locked; maintain a slight bend in the knees.
Exhale and lift your shoulders straight upward toward your ears — move only the shoulders, not the arms or torso.
Pause and squeeze the top of the traps for 1–2 seconds while keeping the neck relaxed (do not jut the chin forward).
Inhale and lower the shoulders back down under control to the starting position; avoid letting the weights drop.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining slow, controlled reps and avoiding any rolling or circular shoulder motion.
If grip is limiting, consider using lifting straps; for variation you can perform seated shrugs to reduce lower body involvement.
exercises.description: Standing dumbbell shrugs raise the shoulders straight up toward the ears to primarily target the trapezius and supporting shoulder/neck muscles. Use a controlled motion with a brief squeeze at the top and avoid rolling the shoulders.

tips: Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
Focus on breathing patterns - exhale on exertion.
Keep transitions between exercises quick.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral (palms facing) grip.

Hinge at the hips until your torso is leaning forward (torso roughly parallel to the floor or about 30–45° above parallel), knees slightly bent; this is a bent-over position — brace your core and keep a neutral spine.
Let the dumbbells hang straight down from your shoulders with your arms fully extended; keep your head aligned with your spine.
Initiate the pull by driving your elbows back and up, pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs or hip area while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, maintaining tightness through the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders excessively.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with controlled motion until your arms are extended.
Repeat for the prescribed reps, keeping the torso fixed (avoid using momentum or excessive torso rotation) and continue to breathe: exhale while pulling, inhale while lowering.
exercises.description: A bent-over row performed with dumbbells where you hinge at the hips and pull the weights toward your torso, emphasizing the lats and upper back. The movement also engages the arms and posterior chain to stabilize the hinged position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.

exercises.name: Cable Bar Lateral Pulldown
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the lat pulldown bench with feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
instructions: Sit upright on the lat pulldown bench with feet flat on the floor and knees secured under the thigh pads.
Reach up and take a wide, pronated (overhand) grip on the cable bar, hands wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with chest up and a slight natural arch in the lower back; lean back slightly at the hips (about 10–20 degrees) to create a stable pulling angle.
Exhale and pull the bar straight down toward the upper chest, driving the elbows down and back and leading the movement with the elbows rather than the hands.
Squeeze the lats and upper back at the bottom of the movement, keeping the shoulders down and avoiding shrugging.
Pause briefly (about 1 second) at the bottom while maintaining tension, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the fully extended starting position with controlled resistance.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand tall with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (standing position).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your body) and wrists straight.
Keep a small bend in the elbows throughout the movement and maintain a neutral spine and chest up.
Exhale and raise both arms out to the sides by lifting at the shoulders, leading with the elbows so the hands finish roughly at shoulder height (arms nearly parallel to the floor).
Avoid shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears; keep the neck long and scapulae stable to minimize excessive trap involvement.
Pause briefly (0–1 second) at the top while maintaining tension in the delts.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly and controlled back to the starting position at your sides.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movement smooth and avoiding momentum or swinging; use lighter weight if form breaks down.

tips: Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.

instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.

exercises.name: Pull-up
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
instructions: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
Stand facing the bar and grasp it with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Mount the machine and kneel on the pad with knees supported and feet either crossed or tucked behind; allow the arms to fully extend so your body hangs straight.
Brace your core, keep your head neutral, and pull your shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement.
Exhale and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down and back, bringing your chin or upper chest toward the bar while squeezing the lats and upper back.
Pause briefly at the top with the scapulae retracted and avoid excessive shrugging of the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower yourself under control until your arms are fully extended and shoulders are not shrugged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; reduce assistance (use a lighter counterweight) as you get stronger to increase difficulty.
exercises.description: Assisted pull-up on a counterweight machine where the user kneels on the support pad and pulls their body up to the bar; this exercise primarily targets the lats while also working the pulling muscles of the arms and upper back.

exercises.name: Cable High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.

Position an incline bench or chest pad in front of the machine so you can sit facing the pulleys with your chest pressed firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor; adjust pad height so torso is supported and arms can reach the handles overhead.
Sit tall with chest against the pad, neutral neck, core braced and shoulders down away from ears; reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with arms nearly extended overhead, scapulae slightly protracted; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and out, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end range.
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a supported chest and no torso rotation.
Slowly release the handles under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the start while keeping tension on the cables and scapulae engaged.
Repeat for desired reps; avoid using momentum, leaning away from the pad, or shrugging the shoulders upward.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.

Sit upright with your back and head firmly against the pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90 degrees.
Reach forward and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip, wrists aligned with forearms and shoulders relaxed away from ears.
Begin with elbows slightly below shoulder height and forearms pointed forward (starting position).
Exhale and press the handles forward and slightly together by extending your arms until nearly straight; avoid locking the elbows at full extension.
Pause briefly at the end of the press while maintaining control and keeping the chest engaged.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position, controlling the weight and stopping when elbows reach roughly the same angle as the start.

Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until the arms are nearly fully extended overhead without locking the elbows.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Rope Crossover Seated Row
exercises.notes: Set the low pulley and load an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat facing the machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Set the low pulley and load an appropriate weight; sit on the bench or seat facing the machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Reach forward and grasp the rope with both hands using a neutral grip (palms facing each other) while keeping the spine neutral and chest open; start with arms fully extended.
Lean the torso slightly forward from the hips (not rounding the lower back) to establish a stable starting position.

Exhale and pull the rope toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and down, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end of the concentric phase.
At the peak contraction, optionally separate the rope ends slightly to increase rear delt and upper-back engagement, hold for 1–2 seconds while maintaining a braced core.
Inhale and slowly extend the arms back to the start position under control, maintaining scapular control and a neutral spine throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions and avoid jerking the torso or using momentum; reduce weight if form breaks down.
exercises.description: A seated cable row performed with a rope attachment that emphasizes scapular retraction and the upper back while also engaging the lats and posterior delts. The lifter sits facing the low pulley and pulls the rope toward the torso, squeezing the shoulder blades together.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.
